payload.intent: Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The durable receipt hydration gap described in the PRD exists in current source (verified)
The existing producer skip is all-or-nothing, so partial-fanout-resume (P2) is net-new work
S2 submit, S5 catch-up, and poll boundaries the PRD reuses all exist in source
PRD does not enable fan-out by default and does not weaken P13/P14/Cursor gates (P6 + Out Of Scope)

criteria: Promises have allowed+forbidden outcomes and public boundary (met)
Anti-goals prevent default-on and gate weakening (met)
Restart/concurrency/cleanup edge behaviors fully specified (deferred to TDD - not met at PRD stage)
evidence: No tests run at PRD stage; issues.md names first RED tests per issue (e.g. test_run_dual_agent_workflow_hydrates_durable_agentic_worker_receipts_before_producer)
accept: PRD problem statement is factually accurate against source; hydration gap and all-or-nothing skip confirmed
accept: promise contracts P1-P6 are well-formed with allowed/forbidden outcomes and a public boundary
accept: anti-goals sound; P6 + Out Of Scope forbid default-on fan-out and gate weakening, matching intent
confidence.rationale: Core factual claims of the PRD were verified directly against source (hydration gap, all-or-nothing skip, reuse primitives present), and the contract is internally consistent with typed allowed/forbidden outcomes. Confidence held below 0.94 because this is a forward-looking product contract: restart-trigger, worker concurrency, and orphan-cleanup staleness are deferred to TDD and not yet proven, and the full pytest suite was not run in-session.

payload.content: PRD for durable resumable agentic fan-out reviewed against source. Core gap claim verified: run_dual_agent_workflow (codex_supervisor_stdio.py:498,588) feeds produce_agentic_worker_receipts only caller receipts with no ledger/.handoff hydration; producer skip is all-or-nothing (agentic_executor.py:87-88) so partial reuse is genuinely new. Reuse primitives S2/S5/poll exist. Promises P1-P6 are well-formed with allowed/forbidden outcomes; default-off and gate-preservation explicitly guarded. ACCEPT to tdd_review.

assumptions_to_verify: TDD defines the concrete restart trigger for a crashed detached job
Hydration dedup-by-receipt-id-and-worker-identity is order-independent and idempotent across repeated resumes
Failed/timeout/hash-mismatch receipts are excluded from min_subagents and required_roles satisfaction
P4 staleness/timeout thresholds for reaping pids are deterministic and test-bounded
contradictions_checked: PRD claim 'producer does not hydrate prior receipts' vs source: confirmed true (codex_supervisor_stdio.py:498,588)
PRD claim 'producer can skip when existing_receipts has subagent receipts' vs source: confirmed (agentic_executor.py:87-88), and confirmed it is all-or-nothing so P2 is not already satisfied
PRD claim it reuses S2/S5/poll vs source: confirmed those symbols exist
PRD claim 'does not enable fan-out by default' vs handoff policy: handoff shows agentic_lead_policy=off / lead_direct, consistent
missing_evidence: No test evidence at PRD stage (expected); the named RED tests are not yet RED
No specification of the restart/watchdog trigger that invokes the hydration path
No explicit handling of two concurrent resume processes racing to spawn the same missing role
critical_review.strongest_objection: The PRD promises crash-restart reuse (P2) and orphan cleanup (P4) but does not specify what detects a crashed detached job or triggers the restart; Finding 2 concedes S2 reattach alone does not restart a crashed job, leaving the entry-point to the resume path implicit.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that hydration already exists in source (would make the PRD redundant -> revise), or that the existing producer already does role-aware partial reuse (would make P2 a no-op -> revise), or a contradiction showing the PRD changes lead_direct defaults / weakens P13 (would force deny). None were found.

evidence_refs.ref: No tests run at PRD stage; issues.md names first RED tests per issue (e.g. test_run_dual_agent_workflow_hydrates_durable_agentic_worker_receipts_before_producer)

PRD does not enable fan-out by default and does not weaken P13/P14/Cursor gates (P6 + Out Of Scope)

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content: TDD plan reviewed against current source. Public-boundary tool symbols, the skip-all producer gap (agentic_executor.py:87-88), and the missing ledger/.handoff hydration at the producer call-site (stdio:594) are all verified real. assumptions_to_verify: At GREEN, hydrated runtime_native receipts must still hash-verify through P13 identically to freshly produced receipts (plan asserts this as regression ISS-1/ISS-6)
Task-level cleanup terminates only timed-out live pids and never in-budget workers (P4 forbidden overreach)
Catch-up returns worker events exactly once and in ascending event id order on repeated reads (P5)
contradictions_checked: PRD blocking-question resolution ('no durable hydration feeds the skip path on resume') vs call-site stdio:594 — consistent, hydration absent
Producer claims to support reuse via existing_receipts vs source returning skip-all on any receipt — consistent with the stated gap
New RED test names claimed as not-yet-written vs grep showing them only in docs/, not tests/ — consistent
missing_evidence: No executed RED run demonstrating each new test fails for the intended reason (expected at this gate)
No explicit named test in the plan for P6 'missing Cursor verdict counted as accept'; it relies on existing regression staying green
critical_review.strongest_objection: Slice 2's RED success condition is disjunctive ('skips all work OR duplicates'), which weakens determinism for a public-boundary RED; a single, specific expected-failure is better engineering. The current code unambiguously returns skipped_existing_receipts (skip-all), so the assertion can and should be pinned.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If any named public-boundary symbol or regression test were absent, if the targeted producer/hydration gaps did not exist in current source, or if a PRD forbidden outcome (respawn passing role, count failed as complete, default-on fan-out, missing-verdict-as-accept) had no corresponding assertion path, I would move to REVISE or DENY.

specialists.name: coverage-forbidden-outcome-reviewer
specialists.objection: Slice 2 RED assertion is disjunctive; catch-up test omits explicit exactly-once/ordering restatement

outcome.summary: TDD plan reviewed against current source. Public-boundary tool symbols, the skip-all producer gap (agentic_executor.py:87-88), and the missing ledger/.handoff hydration at the producer call-site (stdio:594) are all verified real. Tests map cleanly to ISS-1..6 and PRD P1-P6 with explicit forbidden outcomes and named fixtures; new RED tests correctly absent from tests/. Two minor assertion-precision refinements noted, non-blocking. Gate accepts.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

assumptions_to_verify: At GREEN, hydrated runtime_native receipts must still hash-verify through P13 identically to freshly produced receipts (plan asserts this as regression ISS-1/ISS-6)
Task-level cleanup terminates only timed-out live pids and never in-budget workers (P4 forbidden overreach)
Catch-up returns worker events exactly once and in ascending event id order on repeated reads (P5)
contradictions_checked: PRD blocking-question resolution ('no durable hydration feeds the skip path on resume') vs call-site stdio:594 — consistent, hydration absent
Producer claims to support reuse via existing_receipts vs source returning skip-all on any receipt — consistent with the stated gap
New RED test names claimed as not-yet-written vs grep showing them only in docs/, not tests/ — consistent
missing_evidence: No executed RED run demonstrating each new test fails for the intended reason (expected at this gate)
No explicit named test in the plan for P6 'missing Cursor verdict counted as accept'; it relies on existing regression staying green
critical_review.strongest_objection: Slice 2's RED success condition is disjunctive ('skips all work OR duplicates'), which weakens determinism for a public-boundary RED; a single, specific expected-failure is better engineering. The current code unambiguously returns skipped_existing_receipts (skip-all), so the assertion can and should be pinned.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If any named public-boundary symbol or regression test were absent, if the targeted producer/hydration gaps did not exist in current source, or if a PRD forbidden outcome (respawn passing role, count failed as complete, default-on fan-out, missing-verdict-as-accept) had no corresponding assertion path, I would move to REVISE or DENY.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

No gate is weakened: P13/P14/Cursor/S2/S3a/S5 are reused, default policy stays off.

payload.content: tdd_review for detached resumable agentic fan-out. Test plan starts at the supervisor_tool_api boundary (run/submit/catch_up/poll), maps all 6 PRD promises and 6 issue slices to named RED tests, enumerates forbidden outcomes, and preserves default-off + required-policy fail-closed gates. Accept with two non-blocking test refinements.

assumptions_to_verify: S2 idempotent submit already prevents a second Popen on same-token retry (reused, not re-implemented by this plan)
S5 catch_up read allowlist can be extended to the new progress event without weakening read scoping
Replay/P13 hash verification treats a .handoff-hydrated receipt identically to a freshly produced one

missing_evidence: Independent sha256 recomputation of the six planning artifacts (Bash approval not granted; contents read directly instead)

An explicit dedup-across-sources test and an explicit hash-mismatch/tampered-receipt negative test
critical_review.strongest_objection: The durable hydration path can double-count a single worker if the same receipt is present in both the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/, which could falsely satisfy min_subagents or required roles — a fail-closed risk. ISS-1 mandates dedup by receipt id/worker identity, but no named test asserts the both-sources-count-once case.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the plan's RED tests are vacuous (already green against current code), that a named boundary symbol or regression test does not exist, or that hydration as specified can let a failed/duplicated receipt satisfy required policy — any of which would move this to revise.

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

payload.intent: Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Hydration gap confirmed: receipt_payloads built only from caller tool_receipts at codex_supervisor_stdio.py:498
produce_agentic_worker_receipts receives only caller receipts via existing_receipts at :588 — no ledger/.handoff hydration
All 8 plan touch-point files exist on disk
3 named regression tests exist in test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py
Plan slices map 1:1 to PRD promises P1-P6 with first RED tests and dependency order
PRD and TDD grill findings all marked resolved

evidence: Gap diagnosis verified accurate at codex_supervisor_stdio.py:498/588 — no durable hydration before producer
All boundary symbols and touch-point files exist; 3 named regression tests confirmed present
Plan preserves default-off fan-out and P1/P2/P3/P13/P14/Cursor per intent
Flag for execution: two run_dual_agent_workflow boundaries (:434/:3176) — hydrate canonical path
confidence.rationale: Core premise, all referenced symbols/files, and named regression tests verified against the source tree, not receipts. Plan is consistent across PRD->TDD->issues->plan with resolved grill findings and explicit gate-preserving guardrails. Residual: this is a plan (execution correctness not yet proven, by design); artifact hash integrity vs handoff not verified in-session; duplicate workflow boundaries and pid-termination semantics are execution risks.

payload.content: Implementation_plan gate ACCEPTED. The plan's core premise (durable worker-receipt hydration gap) is verified true at source: codex_supervisor_stdio.py:498 builds receipt_payloads only from caller tool_receipts and :588 passes only those to produce_agentic_worker_receipts, with no ledger or .handoff/agentic-workers hydration. All cited boundary symbols and 8 touch-point files exist; 3 named keep-green regression tests exist. Plan is sound, traceable (P1-P6 mapped to named tests+loci), public-boundary-first, and explicitly preserves default-off fan-out and P1/P2/P3/P13/P14/Cursor while reusing S2/S3a/S5.

assumptions_to_verify: Implementer hydrates at the canonical run_dual_agent_workflow boundary (:434), not the wrapper (:3176)
Slice 4 pid termination handles cross-platform liveness/signal semantics without killing in-budget workers
Hydrated .handoff receipts genuinely replay through existing P13 verifier without a parallel validator
contradictions_checked: Intent 'do not weaken gates' vs plan — consistent (guardrails forbid bypassing P1/P2/P3/P13/P14/Cursor)
Intent 'do not enable by default' vs plan — consistent (keeps lead_direct + agentic_lead.policy=off default)
PRD blocking-question resolution vs actual source — confirmed true at :498/:588
Plan 'reuse S2/S3a/S5, do not duplicate' vs touch-point list — consistent
missing_evidence: Artifact sha256 integrity vs handoff packet not verified in-session (Bash approval required)
Full pytest suite not executed in-session
The to-be-written RED tests do not yet exist (expected for a plan gate)
critical_review.strongest_objection: This is a plan, not execution: the plan's correctness (that hydration routed through P13 cannot let stale/failed .handoff receipts satisfy required roles, and that pid cleanup won't kill in-budget workers) is asserted via mitigations and tests-to-be-written, not yet proven by running code.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the gap diagnosis is wrong (hydration already exists), that a cited symbol/file is missing, that the plan weakens a gate or defaults fan-out on, or that a slice depends on a non-existent reuse path.

payload.content:
Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

payload.intent: Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Implementation for detached/resumable fan-out hydration and partial reuse does NOT exist in the current source tree
HEAD is the prior wiring task commit 1429992 with no working-tree code changes
Gate produced no issues, outcome events, tests, or implementation receipts

criteria: source presence/absence of hydration and partial-reuse logic
test coverage referencing this task id
confidence.rationale: Multiple independent evidence types converge: replay snapshot diff_bytes=0 at prior commit, direct source reads showing binary skip and tool-receipt-only hydration, absence of partial-reuse symbols, empty outcome/issues ledgers, and planning-only skill receipts. Slightly below 0.95 because the full pytest suite was not run in-session (Bash needs approval) and a non-checked-out branch was not inspected, though the snapshot pins the working tree to HEAD with no diff.

payload.content: Execution gate has zero implementation. Replay snapshot diff_bytes=0 with HEAD at prior task's commit 1429992; produce_agentic_worker_receipts still binary-skips (agentic_executor.py:87-88) instead of partial role reuse; receipt_payloads hydrated only from in-process tool_receipts (codex_supervisor_stdio.py:498,2632), never from ledger or .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ so the durable hydration gap is unresolved; no tests, no issue/outcome events, skill-receipts cover planning stages only. Deny.

assumptions_to_verify: No separate uncommitted/checked-out branch contains the implementation (snapshot pins working tree to HEAD 1429992 with diff_bytes=0, so unlikely)

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Implementation is complete and correct on inspection
Fan-out remains off by default and no gate is weakened
Partial fan-out reuses completed workers and spawns only missing/failed roles

criteria: Implementation present in working tree (verified via git diff --stat)
Each requirement traced to specific functions/line ordering in live source
Tests contain concrete behavioral assertions, not stubs
Gate not weakened: runtime_native + off-by-default preserved
Remaining risk isolated to un-run tests (operational, not code)
confidence.rationale: All three requirements and gate-integrity claims independently re-verified against live source via grep+read (not memory alone); implementation present and correct on inspection. Confidence capped below 0.9 because the test suite was not executed this session (harness-denied), so GREEN is inferred from substantive test content rather than a live pass.

payload.content: Detached/resumable agentic fan-out fully implemented in working tree (666 insertions, 7 files). All three requirements independently verified against live source: orphan-reaping runtime records + detached survival (agentic_workers.py), durable receipt hydration strictly before the producer (codex_supervisor_stdio.py 551->616), and partial-reuse spawning only missing/failed roles (agentic_executor.py). Fan-out stays off by default; runtime_native evidence grade preserved. ACCEPT on inspection; tests substantive but not executed (pytest harness-denied this session).

assumptions_to_verify: pytest actually passes when run by the supervisor
No double-spawn occurs under a real detached resume (E2E asserts planner_calls==[] but only in test harness)
PID-liveness reap behaves correctly on the live platform
contradictions_checked: Prior replay snapshot showed diff_bytes 0 / HEAD-only DENY — contradicted by live git diff --stat (666 ins) and M status on 7 files; live state wins
Hydration-before-producer ordering — confirmed line 551 precedes line 616, no contradiction
Gate-weakening risk — packet policy off + tests assert runtime_native, no weakening found
missing_evidence: A live pytest pass/fail for the three modified test files
Confirmation the changes are committed (currently uncommitted working-tree state)

critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A pytest run showing any failure in the three modified test files, or discovery that hydration is bypassed when the agentic policy is active, would flip this to REVISE/DENY.

payload.intent: Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

payload.intent: Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the agentic fan-out safe and resumable as a detached job. A long fan-out should run detached, survive an MCP transport drop, and resume without double-spawning workers or orphaning processes. Resolve the durable receipt hydration gap by ensuring prior worker receipts from the ledger and .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/ are visible before produce_agentic_worker_receipts runs; partial fan-outs should reuse completed workers and spawn only missing or failed roles. Reuse S2 idempotent submit, S3a ledger terminal outcome, S5 catch_up, P13/P14 evidence verification, and Cursor review. Do not enable fan-out by default or weaken any gates.

assumptions_to_verify: The 4 new tests pass when actually run
No other test regressed by the state.py event-kind whitelist addition or executor signature change
contradictions_checked: Prior round-1/2 DENY claimed zero implementation via a stale replay snapshot; refuted by live git diff showing 666 insertions
Verified hydration is not after the producer (would be a no-op): confirmed L551 precedes L616
Verified fan-out not enabled by default: handoff packet policy=off, min_subagents=0
missing_evidence: Actual pytest pass/fail output for the 3 affected test files
critical_review.strongest_objection: The test suite has never been executed in any of the 5 review rounds; correctness rests entirely on static inspection, so a latent runtime failure (async fixture, mock mismatch) cannot be excluded.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A pytest run showing any of the 4 new tests failing, or discovery that a gate was weakened or fan-out enabled by default.

Tests exist and assert concrete behavior tied to code paths
